overnight_news: **US non-farm payrolls fell 92,000 in Feb 2026, unemployment at 4.4%** (reported yesterday, last week); signals cooling labor market, boosts rate cut odds but adds volatility to broad US indices.[3]
**Oil surged above $80/barrel** (recent, highest since 2024); raises inflation fears, delays Fed cuts, pressures airlines/travel stocks (e.g., DAL, UAL); Dow dropped >1,000 pts.[3]
**Administration proposed 15% global tariffs**, 20 states suing to block; trade policy swings markets, impacts multinationals (e.g., broad S&P 500).[3]
**DMN (Damon Inc., NASDAQ) pre-market volume 2.23B** (Mar 9, liquidity focus); unusual volume could drive momentum/attention to DMN.[1]
**Indian Sensex tanks >1,800 pts, Nifty <23,900** (Mar 9 session); global risk-off sentiment may spill over to US open, affecting ADRs/emerging exposure tickers.[2]

sunday_thesis.friday_close_summary: Major indices closed sharply lower on Friday, March 6, 2026, amid a bearish selloff driven by geopolitical tensions (Iran conflict boosting oil), weak jobs data, and rising VIX to 23.75. S&P 500 -1.33% to 6740.02[1][2], Nasdaq -1.51%/-1.6% to ~24643/22387[1][2], Dow -0.94% (-453 pts) to 47501.55[1][2], Russell 2000 -2.3%[2]. Utilities (XLU) led with modest gains/decline of -0.35%, Technology (XLK) lagged -2.6%+. Elevated volume signaled institutional selling; worst week since Oct[1][2].

ah_movers.reason: Part of Magnificent 7 pressure amid tech selloff[1]

sunday_thesis.weekend_catalysts: No specific weekend developments detailed in available data. Key ongoing factors: Escalating Iran conflict driving crude oil +12.34% to $91.01 (highest since 2023), pushing inflation fears and delaying Fed rate cut expectations[1][2][3]; 10Y Treasury yield up to 4.14%, USD down to 98.89[1]; defensive sector rotation; VIX surge signaling uncertainty[1].
sunday_thesis.market_bias: Bearish - Risk-off from oil/geopolitics, inflation pressures delaying rate cuts, tech weakness, elevated VIX; watch S&P 6750/6800 support[1][2][3].
